step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the assessment that Reed-Sternberg cells in Hodgkin's lymphoma originate from mature B-cells. One additional point reinforcing this view is the fact that Reed-Sternberg cells express various surface markers typically found on B-cells such as CD19, CD20, and CD79a. While these proteins may also be present in some non-B cell subsets during certain conditions, their consistent expression in Reed-Sternberg cells further solidifies their B-cell lineage origins.

Therefore, my answer choice remains: A.
